info.title: DocRouter Organization RC8
info.description: DocRouter Organization: org-scoped API (paths under /v0/orgs/). Upload documents, OCR/LLM extraction, prompts, schemas, knowledge bases, webhooks. Use an organization API token (org_...) under Settings → Developer. Account-level APIs (/v0/account/) use the separate DocRouter Account connector.

organization_id.name: organization_id
organization_id.description: Placeholder; the connection Organization ID is injected by the connector script. Do not set manually.

document_id.name: document_id
document_id.description: The unique identifier of the document.

prompt_id.name: prompt_id
prompt_id.description: Prompt ID. For GetPrompt, you may instead supply a revision ID in the format {prompt_id}:{version}.

schema_id.name: schema_id
schema_id.description: Schema ID. For GetSchema, you may instead supply a revision ID in the format {schema_id}:{version}.

tag_id.name: tag_id
tag_id.description: The unique identifier of the tag.

kb_id.name: kb_id
kb_id.description: The unique identifier of the knowledge base.

webhook_endpoint_id.name: id
webhook_endpoint_id.description: The webhook endpoint id returned when the trigger was registered. Supplied automatically when unregistering.

document_name.description: Original file name including extension (e.g. invoice.pdf). Supply from a previous step that exposes a file name, or type it manually.

content.description: File bytes as Base64. Use the **file content** output from any connector that reads a file (cloud storage, HTTP, email attachments, etc.), or a data URL (`data:...;base64,...`).

metadata.description: Optional custom fields as a JSON object string: string keys and string values only, e.g. {"InvoiceId":"123","Source":"CRM"}. Use expressions or Compose to build JSON; plain key=value text is not valid. Leave empty if unused.
Optional custom fields as a JSON object string (same format as upload). Leave empty if unused.

id.description: Returned by Get Document. Use as **document_id** on subsequent actions.

document_id.description: Same identifier as **id** on some responses.

type.description: File MIME type when returned by Get Document.

content.description: Present when **Include File Content** is true. Map to any action that writes a file (cloud storage, HTTP, local gateway, etc.): use this field as file body and **document_name** as the target file name where applicable.

id.description: The created webhook endpoint id. Power Automate maps this to the {id} path parameter in the unsubscribe DELETE call.

WebhookEventPayload.description: Payload DocRouter POSTs to the callback URL when an event fires.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event delivery.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the event was fired.

document.description: Present for all document and LLM events.

prompt.description: Present for llm.completed and llm.error events.

llm_output.description: Present for llm.completed events. Contains the extracted data.

error.description: Present for document.error and llm.error events.

tags.name: Triggers
tags.description: Trigger operations — fires a flow when a DocRouter event occurs.

tags.name: Documents
tags.description: Upload, retrieve, update, and delete documents.

tags.name: OCR
tags.description: Run OCR and download text, markdown, or HTML output.

tags.name: LLM
tags.description: Run LLM extraction, retrieve and update results, list models.

tags.name: Prompts
tags.description: Manage prompts that drive LLM extraction.

tags.name: Tags
tags.description: Manage tags used to categorize documents and route them to prompts.

tags.name: Schemas
tags.description: Manage JSON schemas that enforce structured LLM output.

tags.name: Knowledge Bases
tags.description: Manage knowledge bases for semantic search and RAG-augmented prompts.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of documents in the organization, optionally filtered by tag or name.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of records to skip for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

parameters.name: tag_ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of tag IDs to filter documents.

parameters.name: name_search
parameters.description: Filter documents whose name contains this string.

post.description: Upload a single document. The body is one file (file name + Base64 content), not an array—works with any upstream step that provides binary content as Base64. The connector forwards to the DocRouter API as a one-item batch. After upload, OCR and LLM processing are queued automatically.

200.description: OK — returns the uploaded document record (same shape as a single List Documents item).

delete.description: Permanently deletes a document along with its OCR output, LLM results, and file content.

get.description: Returns metadata and optionally the file content (Base64) for a single document. Use **document_id** from **Upload Document** or **List Documents**. To persist the file elsewhere, pass **content** and **document_name** to any connector that creates or updates files (storage, HTTP, on-premises, etc.).

parameters.name: include_content
parameters.description: Set to true to include the Base64-encoded file content in the response.

put.description: Updates the document's display name, tag assignments, or custom metadata fields.

post.description: Triggers OCR (AWS Textract) for a document. Use force=true to re-run.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Set to true to re-run OCR even if results already exist.

parameters.name: ocr_only
parameters.description: Set to true to run OCR without triggering LLM extraction afterwards.

get.description: Returns the plain-text OCR output for a document.

parameters.name: page_num
parameters.description: Page number to retrieve (1-based). Leave blank to return all pages concatenated.

get.description: Returns page count and OCR date.
Returns the document's OCR output rendered as Markdown, preserving headings and layout structure.
Returns the document's OCR output as an HTML page, suitable for embedding or display in a browser.

post.description: Queues LLM processing for a document using the specified prompt.

parameters.name: prompt_revid
parameters.description: Pass a prompt_revid ({prompt_id}:{version}) or 'default'.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Set to true to re-run LLM extraction even if results already exist.

delete.description: Removes the stored LLM extraction result for a document, allowing it to be re-processed.

parameters.name: prompt_revid
parameters.description: Format: {prompt_id}:{version}

get.description: Returns the structured data extracted by the LLM for a document, along with verification status.

parameters.name: prompt_revid
parameters.description: Pass a prompt_revid ({prompt_id}:{version}) or 'default'.

put.description: Allows human reviewers to correct extracted data and mark it as verified.

parameters.name: prompt_revid
parameters.description: Format: {prompt_id}:{version}

get.description: Downloads the LLM extraction result as a JSON file attachment, useful for archiving or passing to downstream systems.
Returns the LLM models enabled for this organization, for use in prompt configuration or direct LLM calls.

parameters.name: chat_only
parameters.description: Set to true to return only chat-capable models, excluding embedding-only models.

post.description: Creates a new prompt for LLM-based data extraction. Optionally link a schema to enforce structured output.

get.description: Returns all prompts in the organization. Each prompt drives LLM extraction for documents matching its assigned tags.

parameters.name: name_search
parameters.description: Filter results whose name contains this string.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of records to skip for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the prompt and all its versions. Supply the prompt_id (not a revid).

get.description: Fetch a specific prompt version. Supply a revision ID in the format {prompt_id}:{version} (e.g. 64a1b2c3:3). To get the latest version, pass just the prompt_id.

parameters.name: prompt_id
parameters.description: Revision ID format: {prompt_id}:{version}. Pass prompt_id alone for latest.

put.description: Creates a new version of the prompt. Supply the prompt_id (not a revid).

get.description: Returns the full version history of a prompt. Each edit creates a new version; older revids remain usable.

post.description: Creates a new tag for categorizing documents and associating them with prompts or forms.

get.description: Returns all tags in the organization. Tags are used to categorize documents and route them to the correct prompts.

parameters.name: name_search
parameters.description: Filter results whose name contains this string.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of records to skip for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

delete.description: Deletes a tag. Documents that carried the tag are not deleted; the tag association is removed.

get.description: Returns the name, color, and description of a single tag.

put.description: Updates a tag's name, color, or description.

post.description: Creates a new JSON schema to enforce structured output on an LLM prompt. Uses standard JSON Schema format.

get.description: Returns all JSON schemas in the organization. Schemas define the structured output shape for LLM extraction prompts.

parameters.name: name_search
parameters.description: Filter results whose name contains this string.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of records to skip for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the schema and all its versions. Supply the schema_id (not a revid).

get.description: Fetch a specific schema version. Supply a revision ID in the format {schema_id}:{version} (e.g. 64a1b2c3:2). To get the latest version, pass just the schema_id.

parameters.name: schema_id
parameters.description: Revision ID format: {schema_id}:{version}. Pass schema_id alone for latest.

put.description: Creates a new version of the schema. Supply the schema_id (not a revid).

post.description: Validate a JSON object against a schema version. Supply a revision ID ({schema_id}:{version}) to validate against a specific version, or just the schema_id for the latest version.

get.description: Returns the full version history of a schema. Each edit creates a new version; older revids remain usable by pinned prompts.

post.description: Creates a new knowledge base. Documents tagged to the KB will be chunked and indexed for semantic search.

get.description: Returns all knowledge bases in the organization. Knowledge bases index documents for semantic search and RAG-augmented prompts.

parameters.name: name_search
parameters.description: Filter results whose name contains this string.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of records to skip for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the knowledge base and all its indexed chunks. Source documents are not deleted.

get.description: Returns configuration and status for a single knowledge base.

put.description: Updates the knowledge base name, description, system prompt, or chunking/embedding settings.

post.description: Semantic search over indexed documents in the knowledge base.
Re-indexes any documents that are out of sync with the knowledge base.
Triggers the flow when DocRouter fires a webhook event (document uploaded, OCR or LLM completed, or error). The connector registers the flow's callback URL; DocRouter posts events to that URL.
post.x-ms-trigger-hint: To verify the trigger, upload a document or use Test in DocRouter webhook settings so a webhook.test event is delivered.

events.description: Events to subscribe to. Leave empty to receive all events.

delete.description: Deregisters the callback URL. Called automatically when the flow is turned off or deleted.
